beats.activity: Eat dinner and relax at home.; Work on poetry notes for next month's open-mic set, refining the piece about routine and invisibility.

(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary: Woke early to that perfect Seattle light filtering through the kitchen window—espresso in hand, I checked the weather and smiled. Seventy-seven degrees, clear skies. Good flying weather. The drive to Sea-Tac was smooth, and the tower hummed with Friday energy. Eight hours of vectors and callsigns, the usual ballet of arrivals and departures. By mid-afternoon my shoulders were tight, but there's something grounding about it, you know? Stopped by Safeway on the way home and grabbed fresh greens—needed something simple tonight. Made a light dinner, then spent time with my poetry notes. That piece about routine and invisibility kept pulling at me; I rewrote two stanzas. Later, heading to Mox in Ballard for board games with the crew felt like exactly what I needed. The city was buzzing with Pride festivities downtown, and even up in Ballard there was this gentle festiveness in the air. Drove home feeling lighter than I have in weeks.
thought_process: Today felt like a good equilibrium—work grounded me as it always does, but I wasn't drained by it. The weather probably helped; there's something about those rare warm, breezy Fridays that shifts my whole temperament. I chose the poetry work over scrolling because that open-mic is coming up and I've been avoiding it, but tonight the words felt ready. The board game night was deliberate too; I've been isolating a bit, and my friends needed me there as much as I needed them. Even though Pride was happening downtown and I wasn't part of that scene, there was something about the city's energy that felt inclusive somehow—like celebration was just happening around me, and that was okay. I'm learning that invisibility doesn't have to mean loneliness.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
